children.name: Sarothrura
children.description: Small, secretive rails with a distinctive fluffy tail that is often cocked upright. Males and females often show striking sexual dimorphism, with males more colourful and females more cryptic. They are found across sub-Saharan Africa and Madagascar.

children.name: Sarothrura pulchra
children.description: A striking flufftail with a bright chestnut head and breast, black upperparts heavily spotted with white, and a short, cocked tail. It inhabits dense undergrowth along forest streams and swamps from Senegal to Uganda and south to Angola. Its call is a low, resonant hoot repeated at intervals.

children.name: Sarothrura elegans
children.description: The most widespread flufftail, found from Ethiopia to South Africa in forested and well-wooded habitats. The male has a rich chestnut head and breast with black upperparts finely spotted with buff, while the female is entirely brown-barred. Its distinctive call is a soft, repeated 'hoot-hoot-hoot' accelerating into a trill.

children.name: Sarothrura rufa
children.description: A small, handsome flufftail with a bright rufous-chestnut head, neck and breast, a black-and-white spotted back and a short black tail. It favours dense marshes and wetlands with tall sedges and grasses across eastern and southern Africa. It is more often heard than seen, its low-pitched drumming call ventriloquial and difficult to locate.

children.name: Sarothrura affinis
children.description: An elusive flufftail with a bright chestnut head and breast and a boldly black-and-white striped back, found in the highland grasslands and moorlands of eastern and southern Africa. It occurs at elevations between 1,500 and 3,000 metres in damp, tussocky habitats. Its call is a distinctive, far-carrying trill that rises in pitch.
